topic: Hotend PID Autotune (M303) and Bed PID (PIDTEMPBED)
summary:
The hotend already runs full PID, but with hardcoded generic Ender-3 constants (Kp 21.73 / Ki 1.54 / Kd 76.55 in Configuration.h lines 688-694), NOT autotuned for the Sprite Extruder Pro direct-drive hotend. The Sprite Pro has a different heater block mass and cartridge than stock, so a fresh M303 E0 autotune is worthwhile, done at the most-used print temperature (e.g. 205-215C for PLA). Procedure: M303 E0 S<temp> C8 U1 (8 cycles, auto-apply), then M500 (writes to EEPROM; EEPROM_SETTINGS is enabled), then M503 to confirm. The U1 flag applies the result to live settings; without it M303 only prints suggested values and you set them manually with M301. Bed PID (PIDTEMPBED) is currently disabled (line 773), so the bed uses bang-bang. The SKR Mini E3 V2.0 uses a proper low-Rds(on) bed MOSFET (WSK220N04), so enabling bed PID is electrically safe here - the Marlin SSR/PWM warning (lines 761-769) does NOT apply to this onboard FET. Enabling it is OPTIONAL: bang-bang is fine and is the Ender-3 default. Bed PID only tightens steady-state regulation; it does NOT heat faster. To enable: uncomment PIDTEMPBED, reflash, autotune with M303 E-1 C8 S60 (start near ambient or it can time out), M500, M503. E-1 = bed, E0 = first hotend. Verification: M303 streams bias/d/Ku/Tu lines per cycle and ends with 'PID Autotune finished' plus new Kp/Ki/Kd; after M500/reboot, M503 echoes values on the M301 line (M304 for bed); in printing the hotend should hold within ~+/-1C with no sustained swings. Config file: /Users/S225608/Documents/dev/Hoverfly/Firmware/Marlin/Configuration.h</summary>
<recommendations>[{"title":"Re-run hotend PID autotune for the Sprite Extruder Pro","priority":"Medium","currentValue":"Kp 21.73, Ki 1.54, Kd 76.55 (generic Ender-3 stock values)","recommendedValue":"Autotuned values from M303 E0 at working temp","rationale":"The hardcoded constants are stock Ender-3 hotend values; the Sprite Pro has different heater-block mass and cartridge, so they are unlikely optimal. Autotuning at the real print temp minimizes temperature swing, which matters for direct-drive consistency and interacts with LIN_ADVANCE quality.","howToApply":"Heat to working temp with part fan at a representative level (M106 S128). Run M303 E0 S210 C8 U1 (use your PLA temp; C8=8 cycles, U1=apply). Wait for 'PID Autotune finished!'. M500 to save, M503 to confirm new P/I/D on the M301 line. Optionally paste new Kp/Ki/Kd into Configuration.h lines 688-694 for reflash persistence.","sources":["https://marlinfw.org/docs/gcode/M303.html","https://3dprintbeginner.com/hotend-and-heatbed-pid-tuning/","https://www.3dmakerengineering.com/blogs/3d-printing/pid-tuning-marlin-firmware"]},{"title":"Leave bed on bang-bang, or optionally enable PIDTEMPBED (safe on this board)","priority":"Low","currentValue":"PIDTEMPBED disabled (line 773) - bed uses bang-bang","recommendedValue":"Keep disabled (acceptable) or enable + autotune for tighter bed regulation","rationale":"Bang-bang is the Ender-3 default and adequate; the SKR Mini E3 V2.0 onboard bed MOSFET (WSK220N04, low Rds-on) makes PWM-based bed PID electrically safe, so the generic Marlin SSR/PWM warning does not apply. Bed PID only improves steady-state stability, not heat-up speed, so benefit for PLA is minor.","howToApply":"If wanted: uncomment '#define PIDTEMPBED' (line 773) and reflash. With bed near room temp run M303 E-1 C8 S60 (E-1=bed). Then M304 to view, M500 to save, M503 to verify. Do not start bed autotune from a target far above ambient or it may time out.","sources":["https://marlinfw.org/docs/gcode/M303.html","https://github.com/bigtreetech/BIGTREETECH-SKR-mini-E3/issues/537","https://3dwork.io/en/6-tricks-to-improve-your-heatbed/","http://www.soliforum.com/topic/1720/massively-improved-bed-temp-stability-if-you-enable-pid/"]},{"title":"Always save with M500 and verify with M503","priority":"High","currentValue":"EEPROM_SETTINGS enabled","recommendedValue":"M500 after every autotune; M503 to confirm","rationale":"M303 (even with U1) only updates live RAM values, lost on reboot unless written with M500. M503 reports stored constants so you can confirm the tune persisted. Skipping M500 is the most common reason a completed tune appears to have no effect after a power cycle.","howToApply":"After any M303: send M500, optionally reboot, then M503 and confirm hotend P/I/D on the M301 line (bed on M304 if enabled). M501 reloads from EEPROM; M502 resets to firmware defaults.","sources":["https://marlinfw.org/docs/gcode/M303.html","https://gist.github.com/matseee/59bf890b975ce405df962ee6c118c9b8","https://support.dremc.com.au/support/solutions/articles/51000266870-pid-calibration"]}]</recommendations>
<procedure>HOTEND PID AUTOTUNE (M303 E0):
1. Connect via a host (OctoPrint/Pronterface/USB terminal). Put the part-cooling fan in a representative state (M106 S128 or S255) since airflow changes thermal response.
2. Run: M303 E0 S210 C8 U1  (E0=first hotend; S210=your real PLA temp e.g. 205-215; C8=8 cycles; U1=auto-apply result).
3. Watch console: each cycle prints bias/d/min/max/Ku/Tu lines; ends with "PID Autotune finished! Put the Kp, Ki and Kd constants..." and the new values.
4. Save: M500
5. Verify: M503 - new values appear on the "M301 P.. I.. D.." line. Optional: copy into Configuration.h DEFAULT_KP/KI/KD lines 688-694.

BED PID (only if you enable it):
1. Uncomment #define PIDTEMPBED (Configuration.h line 773), recompile, flash.
2. With bed near room temp: M303 E-1 C8 S60.
3. After "PID Autotune finished!": M304 to view, M500 to save, M503/M304 to verify.

PASS: completes with "PID Autotune finished!" (no "failed! timeout" / "temperature too high"); after reboot M503 shows new constants; in steady printing hotend holds within ~+/-1C with no sustained sawtooth.
FAIL: tune times out (cold start or target too high vs ambient, mainly a bed issue); "PID not enabled" for bed means PIDTEMPBED not compiled in; temps oscillate >+/-2C after tuning means re-tune at real print temp with correct fan state.</procedure>
<stlModel></stlModel>
